title: Priority elevation when re-queuing after CAD failure
description:
File: python/src/lichen/link/link_layer.py:314-318

In drain_tx_queue(), when CAD fails and a packet must be re-queued, it is given Priority.ROUTING (highest priority) regardless of its original priority:

    try:
        self.tx_queue.push(frame_bytes, priority=Priority.ROUTING)

A BULK packet that fails CAD will be elevated to ROUTING priority, potentially preempting legitimate routing control packets. This violates the priority guarantees.

Fix: Store original priority with frame_bytes or track it separately. Or re-queue with original priority.
close_reason: prevented priority elevation on CAD requeue by preserving original priority; ruff 3x; <3files; conventions followed
